subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 27
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for changing its color to match its surroundings?
questions.explanation: Chameleons have special cells in their skin that allow them to change color for communication and camouflage.
questions.hint: It is a type of lizard.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Germany?
questions.explanation: Berlin is the capital and largest city of Germany.
questions.hint: It was once divided by a famous wall.

questions.q: Which part of the plant holds the seeds and often tastes sweet?
questions.explanation: The fruit develops from the flower and protects the seeds inside; many are delicious and sweet.
questions.hint: You eat these as a snack.

questions.q: What do we call a person who studies the stars and planets?
questions.explanation: An astronomer is a scientist who focuses on celestial objects, space, and the physical universe.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit for your body?
questions.explanation: Water is essential for our body to function correctly, stay hydrated, and keep organs healthy.
questions.hint: It has no sugar or calories.

questions.q: Which community helper helps to keep our teeth healthy?
questions.explanation: A dentist is a professional who checks your teeth and helps prevent cavities.
questions.hint: They use a special chair and bright light.

questions.q: What is the largest continent in the world?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest and most populous continent on Earth.

questions.q: Which of these is a form of energy that we get from the Sun?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is energy generated from sunlight.

questions.q: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.explanation: Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
Winter follows autumn, bringing colder temperatures and sometimes snow.
